page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 vCPUs mapped to 90 physical cores using AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with simultaneous multithreading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 1440.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is provided via 6432 GB of local NVMe SSDs, while no dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. Multi-core CPU execution via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests ranks in the top 10%, supported by advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks also falls within the top 10%, registering read speeds of 13557.95 GB/sec for cached blocks and 346.89 GB/sec for uncached blocks. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench and stress-ng tests, though single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression show average, middle-50% throughput.

This instance demonstrates high resource density, combining massive memory capacity with high-capacity local NVMe storage. It achieves top-tier results in database-adjacent operations, such as Redis SET operations yielding 212.07 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 86.81 GB/sec. Large-scale LLM inference benchmarks also show top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for 7B and 70B models. The server is suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180-lssd is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 180 vCPUs across 90 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores, 1440.0 GB of RAM, and 6432 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Lacking a GPU, it relies on its high-density CPU and memory architecture, which delivers top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in the top 10% of benchmarks. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25%. The server excels in memory-bound and storage-heavy workloads, demonstrating top-tier results in Redis SET operations, static web serving, and LLM inference for medium and large models. It is suited for large-scale databases, data processing pipelines, and CPU-based inference tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180-lssd features 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 1440 GB RAM, and 6432 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 180 vCPUs, 1440 GB RAM, and 6432 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage server delivering top-tier multi-core performance and massive local NVMe capacity for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 180 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with AVX-512 support.
1440.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.
6432 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and CPU-based LLM inference.
